wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the BaseVar2 project overview section focused on the ultra-low-depth whole genome sequencing variant calling tool. Explain its purpose as a specialized C++ implementation for non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT) applications, highlighting the significant performance improvements over the original Python version. Document the core objectives of accurate variant detection and allele frequency calculation from <1x coverage data. Include the citation information and publication details. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining ultra-low-depth sequencing challenges and the mathematical foundations. Use terminology consistent with the bioinformatics and statistical genetics domains. Include practical examples demonstrating the tool's capabilities and target use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Caller Command
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the BaseVar2 caller command, which performs variant calling and allele frequency estimation from ultra-low-depth WGS data. Document the complete command syntax, parameter categories including quality thresholds, batch processing options, threading configuration, and population analysis settings. Provide detailed explanations of each parameter with default values, acceptable ranges, and practical usage scenarios. Include extensive usage examples for single sample analysis, multi-sample population studies, and large-scale batch processing workflows. Document output file formats, VCF generation parameters, and quality control options. Address parameter interactions, performance optimization tips, and troubleshooting common issues. Explain the statistical models used and how different parameters affect variant calling sensitivity and specificity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Likelihood Ratio Testing Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's likelihood ratio testing (LRT) framework. Explain the theoretical foundation of LRT in variant calling, including null and alternative hypotheses formulation. Document the implementation details of the LRT algorithm in the BaseType class, covering the _f() method for calculating population likelihoods across base combinations. Detail the mathematical formulations for likelihood calculations, log-likelihood ratios, and significance thresholds. Explain the LRT_THRESHOLD constant (24 corresponding to p=10^-6) and its biological significance. Include practical examples showing how LRT distinguishes between reference and variant alleles, handles multiple base combinations, and determines variant quality scores. Address numerical stability considerations and computational efficiency optimizations in likelihood calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the BaseVar2 introduction section focused on the tool's core purpose and capabilities. Explain how BaseVar2 serves as a specialized variant calling tool for ultra-low-depth whole genome sequencing data (<1x coverage), particularly targeting non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT) applications. Document the significant performance improvements over the original Python version, highlighting the 10x speed increase and reduced memory requirements. Include the mathematical foundations using maximum likelihood and likelihood ratio models for polymorphism detection and allele frequency calculation. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining ultra-low-depth sequencing challenges and the biological significance of NIPT data analysis. Use terminology consistent with bioinformatics and statistical genetics domains.

wiki_catalogs.name: BAM/CRAM Alignment Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for BaseVar2's BAM/CRAM alignment file processing system. Document the Bam class implementation including file opening modes, compression handling, and index management. Explain the integration with htslib for maximum compatibility and performance. Detail the region-based fetching mechanism, iterator usage, and memory-efficient record processing. Include the BamHeader and BamRecord classes for accessing alignment data. Document error handling, validation procedures, and performance optimization techniques for large-scale genomic data processing. Provide practical examples of opening files, fetching regions, and processing alignment records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the variant calling pipeline orchestration system. Explain the BaseTypeRunner class architecture and its role as the central coordinator for the entire variant calling workflow. Document the pipeline stages including input processing, batch file creation, region-based processing, and output generation. Detail the command-line argument parsing, configuration management, and runtime parameter handling. Explain the integration with thread pools for parallel execution and the smart re-run mechanism for efficient batch processing. Include the relationship between different pipeline components and their data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for BaseVar2 focused on installation and initial usage. Document both CMake and manual installation methods with step-by-step instructions. Explain system requirements including C++17 compiler and necessary dependencies. Provide detailed installation verification procedures and basic usage examples. Include prerequisite knowledge for users unfamiliar with bioinformatics tools. Document common installation issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide quick start examples for variant calling from BAM files with different scenarios. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring experienced users can quickly set up and begin analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Concat Command
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the BaseVar2 concat command used to concatenate VCF files from the same set of samples generated by BaseVar. Document the command syntax, input file requirements, and output file generation process. Explain how the concatenation handles sample consistency checks, chromosome order preservation, and metadata merging. Provide usage examples for combining results from different genomic regions, batch processing outputs, and multi-center study integrations. Document file format requirements, error handling for inconsistent inputs, and validation procedures. Include best practices for managing large concatenated datasets and optimizing downstream analysis workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Expectation-Maximization Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm implementation used for allele frequency estimation. Explain the EM algorithm's role in estimating population allele frequencies from ultra-low-depth sequencing data. Document the iterative optimization process, including E-step (expectation) and M-step (maximization) calculations. Detail the convergence criteria and stopping conditions used in the implementation. Explain how the EM algorithm handles missing data and uncertainty in low-coverage scenarios. Include mathematical formulations for the log-likelihood function maximization and parameter updates. Provide examples showing EM algorithm convergence behavior with different initializations and data characteristics. Address computational complexity and performance considerations for large-scale population analyses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Citation and Publication Information
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the citation section focused on proper academic attribution for BaseVar2 research. Document the primary publication reference in Cell Genomics (2024) by Huang et al. including the full citation details, DOI link, and journal information. Explain the research significance of utilizing non-invasive prenatal test sequencing data for human genetic investigation. Include guidelines for citing BaseVar2 in published works, research papers, and academic presentations. Provide information about the collaborative research team and institutional affiliations mentioned in the publication. Address how researchers should acknowledge both the BaseVar2 tool and the underlying methodology when reporting results obtained through its use.

wiki_catalogs.name: FASTA Reference Genome Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's FASTA reference genome file processing system. Document the implementation of reference sequence retrieval, indexing, and sequence extraction. Explain the integration with htslib's faidx functionality for efficient random access to reference sequences. Detail the memory management strategies for large reference genomes, sequence caching mechanisms, and coordinate conversion between different reference assemblies. Include validation procedures for reference files, error handling for missing sequences, and performance optimization techniques for repeated sequence access patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quality Control and Filtering
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the quality control and filtering mechanisms in the variant calling engine. Document the base quality filtering system, mapping quality thresholds, and minimum allele frequency settings. Explain the base quality score handling, including the conversion from ASCII characters to numeric scores. Detail the filtering criteria for reads, bases, and variants. Document the quality metrics calculation and validation processes. Include the relationship between quality parameters and variant calling accuracy. Address common quality control scenarios and their impact on downstream analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Command-Line Interface Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive CLI documentation for BaseVar2's command-line interface. Document all three main commands: 'caller', 'concat', and 'subsam' with complete syntax, parameters, and usage examples. For each command, provide detailed parameter descriptions, default values, and practical usage scenarios. Include the caller command's extensive parameter set covering quality thresholds, batch processing, threading, and population analysis options. Document output formats and file handling. Provide real-world usage examples for different analysis scenarios including single sample, multi-sample, and large-scale batch processing. Address parameter interactions and best practices for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Subsam Command
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the BaseVar2 subsam command that extracts variants for specified samples from VCF files and outputs new VCF files. Document the command syntax, sample specification methods, and output file generation. Explain how the tool handles sample name matching, missing samples, and output file formatting. Provide usage examples for extracting subsets for population studies, quality control analyses, and targeted variant screening. Document input file requirements, error handling for invalid sample names, and validation procedures for extracted datasets. Include best practices for sample selection strategies and downstream analysis preparation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statistical Models and Probability Calculations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's statistical models and probability calculation framework. Explain the probabilistic models underlying variant detection, including base error models and mapping quality considerations. Document the probability calculations for different genotypes and allelic states. Detail the integration of quality scores, mapping qualities, and base depths in statistical inference. Explain the role of prior probabilities and how they influence posterior probability calculations. Cover the mathematical foundations of the Bayesian framework used for variant calling. Include formulas for calculating posterior probabilities, marginal likelihoods, and Bayes factors. Document the handling of uncertainty quantification and confidence interval estimation. Address model assumptions, limitations, and validation approaches for statistical model accuracy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Characteristics and Advantages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the performance characteristics section focused on BaseVar2's computational advantages over previous implementations. Document the significant performance improvements achieved through the C++ implementation, including the 10x speed increase compared to the original Python version. Detail the memory efficiency improvements, showing how the C++ version requires only 3-4GB per thread versus over 20GB for the Python version. Explain the batch processing optimizations and thread pool efficiency. Include specific configuration examples showing optimal settings for different data sizes and computational environments. Document the scalability characteristics and performance benchmarks for various input sizes and processing scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: VCF Output Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for BaseVar2's VCF (Variant Call Format) output generation system. Document the VCF writer implementation including header construction, record formatting, and data type handling. Explain the integration with htslib for standardized VCF output and compression support. Detail the VCFHeader and VCFRecord classes for managing metadata and variant information. Include the implementation of INFO, FORMAT, and genotype field handling, quality score formatting, and variant filtering output. Document error handling, validation procedures, and performance optimization for large-scale variant output processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Statistical Analysis Core
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the statistical analysis core of the variant calling engine. Explain the likelihood ratio testing implementation, expectation-maximization algorithm, and maximum likelihood estimation methods. Document the BaseType class functionality including batch information processing, position-based analysis, and variant detection algorithms. Detail the mathematical foundations underlying the statistical models and their application to ultra-low-depth sequencing data. Include the relationship between statistical models and biological interpretations. Address numerical stability considerations and convergence criteria used in the analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Algorithm Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive algorithm documentation for BaseVar2's core statistical models and mathematical foundations. Explain the likelihood ratio testing framework, maximum likelihood estimation methods, and expectation-maximization algorithm implementation. Document the statistical model architecture including probability calculations, hypothesis testing, and allele frequency estimation. Detail the mathematical background and theoretical foundations underlying the variant calling approach. Include algorithmic flowcharts and mathematical formulations. Explain the relationship between different algorithmic components and their integration within the overall pipeline. Address numerical stability considerations and computational efficiency optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mathematical Foundations and Background
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the mathematical foundations underlying BaseVar2's variant calling approach. Explain the theoretical background of maximum likelihood estimation, hypothesis testing, and population genetics principles applied in the tool. Document the mathematical relationships between observed data (base counts, qualities) and inferred parameters (allele frequencies, genotypes). Detail the optimization problems solved, including constrained optimization with inequality constraints. Explain the connection between phred quality scores and error probabilities, including the MLN10TO10 conversion factor. Cover the combinatorial mathematics involved in exploring base combinations and the computational challenges of exact enumeration. Include derivations of key mathematical formulas and their biological interpretations. Address numerical analysis aspects including floating-point precision, overflow/underflow handling, and algorithmic stability considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mathematical Foundations and Theory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the mathematical foundations section focused on the statistical and computational methods underlying BaseVar2. Explain the maximum likelihood estimation approach used for variant detection and the likelihood ratio testing framework. Document the Expectation-Maximization algorithm implementation for allele frequency calculation. Detail the mathematical models for handling ultra-low-depth sequencing data and the statistical corrections applied for rare variant detection. Include the theoretical background for why traditional variant calling approaches fail with <1x coverage data and how BaseVar2's methodology addresses these challenges. Provide references to the comprehensive documentation mentioned in the README for in-depth mathematical explanations.

wiki_catalogs.name: BGZF Compression and Streaming
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's BGZF (Blocked GNU Zip Format) compression and streaming system. Document the implementation of compressed file I/O operations, block-based compression/decompression, and streaming capabilities. Explain the integration with htslib's BGZF functions for maximum compatibility with bioinformatics standards. Detail the memory management for compressed streams, buffer handling, and performance optimization techniques. Include error handling for corrupted compressed files, decompression validation, and best practices for handling large compressed datasets efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: Batch Processing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the batch processing system architecture. Explain the batch file creation mechanism, temporary file management, and batch-based processing workflow. Document the batch size optimization, memory management strategies, and disk I/O efficiency techniques. Detail the batch file format, header information, and data serialization methods. Explain the smart re-run functionality that prevents redundant processing and optimizes resource utilization. Include the relationship between batch processing and parallel execution patterns. Address batch file cleanup, error handling, and recovery mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Variant Calling Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for BaseVar2's variant calling engine architecture and workflow. Explain the pipeline orchestration, input processing, quality control mechanisms, and statistical analysis implementation. Document the integration of alignment data processing, base quality filtering, and variant calling algorithms. Detail the batch processing strategy, memory management, and parallel execution patterns. Include the relationship between different engine components and their data flow. Explain the caller utility functions and helper algorithms. Address performance optimization techniques and resource management strategies used in the engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: Parallel Execution Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the parallel execution framework used in variant calling. Explain the thread pool implementation, task distribution strategies, and load balancing mechanisms. Document the parallelization approach for batch processing, region-based analysis, and statistical computations. Detail the thread safety considerations, shared resource management, and synchronization mechanisms. Explain the performance optimization techniques including work stealing, dynamic load balancing, and memory locality optimization. Include the relationship between thread pool configuration and system resource utilization. Address debugging parallel execution issues and performance profiling techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Format I/O System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's file format input/output system. Document the implementation of BAM/CRAM alignment file processing, FASTA reference genome handling, and VCF output generation. Explain the integration with htslib for maximum compatibility and performance. Detail the stream processing capabilities, compression support, and memory-efficient file handling. Include the internal data structures used for file representation and the conversion between different formats. Document error handling, validation procedures, and performance optimization techniques for large file processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Parallel Processing Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for BaseVar2's parallel processing framework and thread pool implementation. Explain the custom thread pool architecture, load balancing strategies, and task distribution mechanisms. Document the parallel execution patterns used throughout the variant calling pipeline. Detail memory management in multi-threaded environments and synchronization mechanisms. Include performance tuning guidelines, optimal thread count recommendations, and resource allocation strategies. Address thread safety considerations and concurrent data access patterns used in the implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Population Genetics Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's population genetics analysis capabilities. Explain the population group analysis functionality, allele frequency calculation methods, and demographic structure analysis. Document the sample grouping mechanisms and statistical approaches used for population-specific variant calling. Detail the input format requirements for population group files and the interpretation of population-specific results. Include practical examples of population analysis workflows and output interpretation guidelines. Address the statistical significance testing and confidence interval calculations used in population genetics applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Functions and Helpers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for BaseVar2's utility functions and helper components. Document the mathematical and statistical utility functions, data structure implementations, and common algorithm implementations used throughout the codebase. Explain the utility functions for file processing, data manipulation, and algorithm support. Detail the implementation of specialized mathematical functions, statistical distributions, and computational routines. Include the helper functions for data validation, error handling, and performance optimization. Provide usage examples and integration patterns for these utility components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Pipeline Generation and Automation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's pipeline generation and automation tools. Explain the batch processing workflow creation capabilities, large-scale analysis automation, and region-specific processing strategies. Document the pipeline generation script functionality, parameter optimization, and workflow customization options. Detail the integration with cluster computing environments and batch job submission systems. Include practical examples of pipeline creation for different analysis scenarios and performance optimization techniques. Address workflow monitoring, error handling, and result aggregation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: VCF Processing Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for BaseVar2's VCF processing tools and utilities. Document the VCF concatenation functionality for merging multiple VCF files from the same sample sets, and the sample subset extraction capabilities for reducing VCF datasets. Explain the file format conversion utilities and quality control validation procedures. Detail the implementation of VCF manipulation operations and their integration with the main variant calling pipeline. Include practical examples of VCF processing workflows and best practices for data management and quality assurance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for BaseVar2's testing framework and validation procedures. Document the unit test methodology, integration test procedures, and performance benchmarking approaches. Explain the test data organization, test case design, and validation criteria used for quality assurance. Detail the automated testing workflows and continuous integration practices. Include guidelines for contributors on writing and running tests. Address performance regression testing and validation of critical algorithms and data structures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build System and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for BaseVar2's build system and configuration management. Document the CMake build configuration, dependency management, and platform-specific considerations. Explain the integration with htslib and the embedded bioinformatics library setup. Detail the compilation process, linking requirements, and runtime dependencies. Include troubleshooting guides for common build issues and platform-specific installation problems. Address development environment setup, compiler requirements, and optimization flags used in the build process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Developer Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive developer documentation for BaseVar2 focused on code organization, contribution guidelines, and development workflow. Document the codebase architecture, coding conventions, and design patterns used throughout the project. Explain the development environment setup, debugging techniques, and testing procedures for contributors. Detail the version control practices, pull request guidelines, and code review processes. Include guidelines for extending the codebase, adding new features, and maintaining backward compatibility. Address performance profiling, memory management, and optimization techniques for C++ development.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# BaseVar2: Ultra-Low-Pass WGS Variant Calling Tool

## Project Introduction

BaseVar2 is a specialized C++ tool designed for variant calling using ultra-low-depth (<1x) sequencing data, particularly targeting non-invasive prenatal test (NIPT) data in human genetic studies. The tool leverages maximum likelihood and likelihood ratio models to accurately identify polymorphisms at genomic positions and calculate allele frequencies.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Ultra-Low Depth Analysis**: Optimized for sequencing data with coverage below 1x, making it ideal for NIPT applications
- **High Performance**: C++ implementation delivering 10x+ speed improvement over the original Python version
- **Memory Efficiency**: Significantly reduced memory footprint compared to existing solutions
- **Population Genetics**: Supports allele frequency calculations for specific population groups

### Target Audience

- Human genetic researchers studying NIPT data
- Population genetics studies requiring ultra-low-depth analysis
- Bioinformatics pipelines handling large-scale variant calling tasks

## Technical Architecture

BaseVar2 follows a modular architecture with clear separation between I/O operations, core algorithms, and user interface components.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Entry Point] --> B[Command Parser]
    B --> C[Variant Caller]
    B --> D[VCF Concatenator]
    B --> E[VCF Subsetter]
    
    C --> F[BAM/CRAM Reader]
    C --> G[Fasta Reader]
    C --> H[BaseType Algorithm]
    C --> I[Thread Pool]
    
    F --> J[htslib Integration]
    G --> K[Sequence Processing]
    H --> L[Likelihood Ratio Test]
    H --> M[EM Algorithm]
    
    I --> N[Parallel Processing]
    N --> O[Batch Processing]
    
    P[VCF Writer] --> Q[VCF Header]
    P --> R[VCF Records]
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style C fill:#f3e5f5
    style F fill:#e8f5e8
    style H fill:#fff3e0
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

1. **Application Layer**: Main entry point and command-line interface
2. **I/O Layer**: File format handling (BAM/CRAM/VCF/Fasta)
3. **Algorithm Layer**: Core variant calling and statistical analysis
4. **Utility Layer**: Supporting functions and data structures

### Design Patterns

- **Factory Pattern**: Used for creating different file reader/writer instances
- **Strategy Pattern**: Multiple alignment quality assessment strategies
- **Observer Pattern**: Progress monitoring and logging throughout the pipeline
- **Template Pattern**: Standardized processing workflows for different file formats

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application starts from the main entry point with three primary commands:

- **caller**: Primary variant calling functionality
- **concat**: Merge multiple VCF files from the same sample set
- **subsam**: Extract specific samples from existing VCF files

### Core Modules

#### Variant Calling Engine (`src/variant_caller.h/cpp`)
The central component orchestrating the entire variant calling process:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Main
    participant Caller
    participant ThreadPool
    participant Algorithms
    
    User->>+Main: basevar caller [options]
    Main->>+Caller: Initialize with parameters
    Caller->>+ThreadPool: Create worker threads
    ThreadPool->>+Algorithms: Process genomic regions
    Algorithms->>Algorithms: Apply LRT and EM algorithms
    Algorithms-->>ThreadPool: Return variant calls
    ThreadPool-->>Caller: Aggregate results
    Caller-->>Main: Write final VCF
    Main-->>User: Complete execution
```

#### Base Type Algorithm (`src/basetype.h`)
Implements the core statistical model for variant detection:

- Maximum likelihood estimation for allele frequencies
- Likelihood ratio testing for variant significance
- Expectation-Maximization (EM) algorithm for population structure

#### I/O Abstraction (`src/io/`)
Comprehensive file format handling:

- **BAM/CRAM Processing**: Through htslib integration
- **FASTA Sequence Handling**: Reference genome management
- **VCF Generation**: Output format compliance
- **BGZF Compression**: Efficient file compression

### Configuration Approach

The system uses a hierarchical configuration system:

1. **Command Line Arguments**: Primary parameter specification
2. **Default Values**: Conservative defaults for safety
3. **Runtime Validation**: Parameter validation and sanitization
4. **Environment Detection**: Automatic hardware thread detection

### External Dependencies

BaseVar2 integrates with several key external libraries:

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[BaseVar2] --> B[htslib]
    A --> C[CMake]
    A --> D[POSIX Threads]
    A --> E[Standard C++ Library]
    
    B --> F[BAM/CRAM/VCF Processing]
    C --> G[Build System]
    D --> H[Parallel Execution]
    E --> I[Standard Algorithms]
    
    style A fill:#ffebee
    style B fill:#e8f5e8
    style D fill:#e3f2fd
```

**Key Dependencies**:
- **htslib**: Core bioinformatics file format support
- **CMake**: Cross-platform build system
- **POSIX Threads**: Multi-threading support
- **Standard C++17**: Modern C++ features

### Integration Points

The tool provides several integration mechanisms:

1. **Pipeline Generator**: Automated workflow creation for large-scale analysis
2. **Batch Processing**: Efficient handling of multiple samples
3. **Population Grouping**: Structured analysis by demographic categories
4. **Region-Specific Analysis**: Targeted variant calling for specific genomic intervals

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

BaseVar2 offers comprehensive variant calling capabilities optimized for ultra-low-depth data:

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialization
    Initialization --> ParameterValidation
    ParameterValidation --> RegionLoading
    RegionLoading --> SampleProcessing
    SampleProcessing --> AlgorithmExecution
    AlgorithmExecution --> ResultGeneration
    ResultGeneration --> [*]
    
    SampleProcessing --> BatchCreation
    BatchCreation --> ParallelProcessing
    ParallelProcessing --> AlgorithmExecution
    
    AlgorithmExecution --> QualityFiltering
    QualityFiltering --> OutputGeneration
```

### Implementation Highlights

#### High-Performance Computing (`src/external/thread_pool.h`)
- Dynamic thread pool management
- Load balancing across genomic regions
- Memory-efficient parallel processing

#### Statistical Algorithms (`src/algorithm.h`)
- Fisher's exact test implementation
- Chi-square goodness-of-fit testing
- Normal distribution calculations
- Custom EM algorithm for population genetics

#### File Format Support (`src/io/`)
- Native htslib integration for maximum compatibility
- Automatic format detection and conversion
- Streaming I/O for large file handling

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Ultra-Low Depth Data] --> B[Quality Control]
    B --> C[Alignment Processing]
    C --> D[Statistical Analysis]
    D --> E[Variant Calling]
    E --> F[Allele Frequency Estimation]
    F --> G[VCF Output]
    
    H[Population Groups] --> D
    I[Genomic Regions] --> C
    J[Batch Processing] --> C
    
    style A fill:#ffecb3
    style G fill:#c8e6c9
    style D fill:#e1bee7
```

The tool excels in handling the unique challenges of ultra-low-depth sequencing data, including:

- **Noise Reduction**: Advanced filtering to minimize false positives
- **Coverage Optimization**: Efficient processing with minimal data
- **Scalability**: Handles thousands of samples in parallel
- **Accuracy**: Maintains high sensitivity and specificity for rare variants

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [CMakeLists.txt](CMakeLists.txt)
- [src/main.cpp](src/main.cpp)
- [src/variant_caller.h](src/variant_caller.h)
- [src/variant_caller.cpp](src/variant_caller.cpp)
- [src/io/bam.h](src/io/bam.h)
- [src/basetype.h](src/basetype.h)
- [src/algorithm.h](src/algorithm.h)
- [scripts/create_pipeline.py](scripts/create_pipeline.py)
- [src/concat.h](src/concat.h)
- [src/vcf_subset_samples.h](src/vcf_subset_samples.h)

</blog>
